info.title: UOR Framework Agent API
info.summary: A complete, agent-navigable REST API for the Universal Object Reference (UOR) Framework — strictly mapped to the formal ontology at github.com/UOR-Foundation/UOR-Framework.
info.description:
## Overview

This API exposes the full UOR kernel as REST operations. Every endpoint, schema field, and enum value maps 1:1 to a named class, property, or individual in the Rust ontology source under `spec/src/namespaces/`.

The UOR Framework is structured in three spaces:

| Space  | Namespaces                                         | API group        |
|--------|----------------------------------------------------|------------------|
| Kernel | `u:`, `schema:`, `op:`                             | `/kernel`        |
| Bridge | `resolver:`, `partition:`, `observable:`, `proof:`,| `/bridge`        |
|        | `derivation:`, `trace:`, `cert:`                   |                  |
| User   | `type:`, `morphism:`, `state:`                     | `/user`          |

The end-to-end resolution cycle (test7_end_to_end.rs) is:
```
Context → Type → Resolver → Partition → Observable → Cert → Trace → Transform
```

## Quick Start

GET /kernel/op/verify?x=42 — verify neg(bnot(42)) = succ(42) = 43

## Security

All GET endpoints require no authentication. POST endpoints accept optional `X-UOR-Agent-Key` header for rate-limit tier elevation. Rate limits (unauthenticated): 120 req/min for kernel GET, 60 req/min for bridge POST.

## POST Semantics

All POST endpoints return `200 OK` (not `201`) because they compute deterministic artifacts on-the-fly — same inputs always produce the same output. No resource is persisted.

servers.description: Live API — active endpoint (all requests go here)

externalDocs.description: UOR Framework GitHub source and conformance suite

tags.name: kernel-op
tags.description:
**`op/` namespace** — Ring operations, involutions, and algebraic identities.
Source: `spec/src/namespaces/op.rs`
Classes: `Operation`, `UnaryOp`, `BinaryOp`, `Involution`, `Identity`, `Group`, `DihedralGroup`.
Named individuals: `neg`, `bnot`, `succ`, `pred`, `add`, `sub`, `mul`, `xor`, `and`, `or`, `criticalIdentity`, `D2n`.

tags.name: kernel-schema
tags.description:
**`schema/` + `u/` namespaces** — Ring substrate, term language, datum values, and content addressing.
Source: `spec/src/namespaces/schema.rs`, `spec/src/namespaces/u.rs`

tags.name: bridge-partition
tags.description:
**`partition/` namespace** — Irreducibility partition of the ring.
Source: `spec/src/namespaces/partition.rs`
Classes: `Partition`, `IrreducibleSet`, `ReducibleSet`, `UnitSet`, `ExteriorSet`.

tags.name: bridge-proof
tags.description:
**`proof/` namespace** — Verification proofs.
Source: `spec/src/namespaces/proof.rs`
Classes: `Proof`, `CriticalIdentityProof`, `CoherenceProof`, `WitnessData`.

tags.name: bridge-cert
tags.description:
**`cert/` namespace** — Attestation certificates.
Source: `spec/src/namespaces/cert.rs`
Classes: `Certificate`, `TransformCertificate`, `IsometryCertificate`, `InvolutionCertificate`.

tags.name: bridge-observable
tags.description:
**`observable/` namespace** — Metric and geometric measurements.
Source: `spec/src/namespaces/observable.rs`
Classes: `RingMetric`, `HammingMetric`, `CascadeLength`, `CatastropheThreshold`, `Commutator`, `Monodromy`.

tags.name: bridge-derivation
tags.description:
**`derivation/` namespace** — Auditable step-by-step operation records.
Source: `spec/src/namespaces/derivation.rs`

tags.name: bridge-trace
tags.description:
**`trace/` namespace** — Bit-level execution traces for injection detection.
Source: `spec/src/namespaces/trace.rs`

tags.name: bridge-resolver
tags.description:
**`resolver/` namespace** — Canonical form classification of ring elements.
Source: `spec/src/namespaces/resolver.rs`

tags.name: bridge-sparql
tags.description: **SPARQL 1.1** — query and federation endpoints over the UOR knowledge graph.

tags.name: bridge-shacl
tags.description: **SHACL** — W3C Shapes Constraint Language validation over the UOR ontology graph.

tags.name: bridge-emit
tags.description: **JSON-LD emission** — emit UOR datums, derivations, and coherence proofs as valid W3C JSON-LD 1.1 documents.

tags.name: bridge-gnn
tags.description: **Graph Neural Network** — export the UOR knowledge graph in GNN-ready formats for ML pipelines.

tags.name: user-type
tags.description:
**`type/` namespace** — Runtime type declarations.
Source: `spec/src/namespaces/type_.rs`

tags.name: user-morphism
tags.description:
**`morphism/` namespace** — Structure-preserving maps between ring sizes.
Source: `spec/src/namespaces/morphism.rs`

tags.name: user-state
tags.description:
**`state/` namespace** — Agent lifecycle state binding.
Source: `spec/src/namespaces/state.rs`

tags.name: navigate
tags.description: Framework navigation — reading order, namespace index, ontology links, all endpoint URLs.

tags.name: store
tags.description: UOR × IPFS persistent storage. Serialises UOR objects to JSON-LD, computes dual addresses (u:Address via Braille bijection + CIDv1 via sha2-256 multihash), and stores to IPFS.

tags.name: pod-storage
tags.description: **Personal Object Data (POD)** — agent-scoped persistent key-value store backed by UOR content-addressing. Contexts, bindings, and frames.

tags.name: agent-tools
tags.description: **Five canonical agent tool functions** (§6.4) — uor_derive, uor_query, uor_verify, uor_correlate, uor_partition. All endpoints return epistemic_grade.

tags.name: morphism
tags.description: **Morphism Transform API** (§5) — structure-preserving maps between rings.

tags.name: certificates
tags.description: **Certificate Chains** — issue cert:TransformCertificate and GDPR Article 20 Verifiable Credentials.

tags.name: sparql
tags.description: **SPARQL** — query endpoints and federation cardinality planning for the UOR knowledge graph.

tags.name: resolver
tags.description: **Entity Resolution** — natural-language entity resolver using dihedral factorization strategy.

tags.name: semantic-web
tags.description: **Semantic Web Surface** — schema.org extension with UOR derivation attribution.

tags.name: discovery
tags.description: **Discovery** — VoID dataset descriptors, .well-known endpoints, and Linked Open Data registration.

tags.name: oracle
tags.description: **Oracle Ledger** — immutable audit log of all UOR store operations with single-source-of-truth guarantees.

tags.name: observer
tags.description: **Observer Theory** — agent registration, zone classification (COHERENCE/DRIFT/COLLAPSE), and network convergence analysis.

tags.name: graph
tags.description: **Knowledge Graph** — pre-computed Q0 instance graph with all 2^n datums, SPARQL verification, and per-datum lookup.

tags.name: attribution
tags.description: **Attribution** — register derivation provenance, verify attribution chains, and compute royalty reports.

tags.name: ontology
tags.description: **Ontology** — full OWL/RDFS ontology export for the UOR framework.

tags.name: test
tags.description: **End-to-end tests** — runtime conformance verification endpoint.

get.summary: Framework navigation index — reading order, all endpoints, namespace map
get.description: Returns a complete navigation index of the UOR Framework API. Start here to discover all endpoints, namespace mappings, and reading order. No parameters required.

200.description: Complete navigation index with all endpoint URLs and reading order

get.summary: Inline OpenAPI 3.1.0 specification
get.description: Returns the OpenAPI 3.1.0 specification for this API. The full static spec is also available at https://uor.foundation/openapi.json

301.description: Permanent redirect to canonical static spec at https://uor.foundation/openapi.json

get.summary: Verify op:criticalIdentity — neg(bnot(x)) = succ(x)
get.description:
Verifies the foundational theorem of the UOR kernel for a given input x.

**Framework mapping:**
- Individual: `op:criticalIdentity` (type `op:Identity`)
- Properties checked: `op:lhs = op:succ`, `op:rhs = [op:neg, op:bnot]`, `op:forAll = "x ∈ R_n"`
- Proof type produced: `proof:CriticalIdentityProof`
- Witness type: `proof:WitnessData`

**Source:** `spec/src/namespaces/op.rs`, `conformance/src/tests/fixtures/test6_critical_identity.rs`

200.description: Critical identity verification with proof and witness

get.summary: Verify op:criticalIdentity for all 2^n elements of R_n
get.description:
Runs `neg(bnot(x)) = succ(x)` for every x in Z/(2^n)Z and returns a `proof:CoherenceProof`.

When `expand=false` (default), only summary counts are returned. When `expand=true`, the full `proof:WitnessData` array is included.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: Include all witness rows when true (~14 KB for n=8)

200.description: Universal coherence proof for the full ring

get.summary: Compute all op:UnaryOp and op:BinaryOp values for a datum
get.description:
Returns the result of every named op/ individual applied to x.

**UnaryOp individuals:** neg (ring_reflection), bnot (hypercube_reflection), succ (rotation), pred (rotation_inverse)

**BinaryOp individuals:** add (translation), sub, mul (scaling), xor (hypercube_translation), and (hypercube_projection), or (hypercube_join)

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Second operand for binary operations. Must satisfy 0 ≤ y < 2^n. Defaults to x if omitted.

200.description: All ring operations for x (and binary ops for x,y)

get.summary: List all named op/ individuals with full metadata
get.description: Returns the complete catalogue of all 12 operation individuals defined in `op.rs`: neg, bnot, succ, pred, add, sub, mul, xor, and, or, criticalIdentity, D2n.

post.summary: Encode content as a u:Address with u:Glyph decomposition
post.description:
Encodes an arbitrary UTF-8 string as a UOR content address using the 6-bit bijection chr(0x2800 + (b & 0x3F)).

Returns `200 OK` because this endpoint computes a deterministic artifact on-the-fly — same inputs always produce the same output. No resource is persisted.

200.description: u:Address with full u:Glyph decomposition per byte

get.summary: Get full schema:Datum representation for a ring value
get.description:
Returns a complete `schema:Datum` object for a given integer value at quantum level n.

**Framework mapping (schema.rs):**
- `schema:value` — integer value in [0, 2^n)
- `schema:quantum` — the ring's bit-width n
- `schema:stratum` — popcount of set bits
- `schema:spectrum` — binary representation string
- `schema:glyph` — linked `u:Address`

post.summary: Resolve a type:TypeDefinition to a partition:Partition of R_n
post.description:
Applies the partition map Π : T_n → Part(R_n) to produce a four-component partition of the ring.

Returns `200 OK` because this endpoint computes a deterministic artifact on-the-fly — same inputs always produce the same output. No resource is persisted.

**Two modes:** Pass `type_definition` for full ring partition, or `input` string for per-byte analysis.

get.summary: Produce a proof:CriticalIdentityProof for a given witness x
get.description: Returns a fully structured `proof:CriticalIdentityProof` linking back to `op:criticalIdentity` via `proof:provesIdentity`.

post.summary: Produce a proof:CoherenceProof for a type:TypeDefinition
post.description:
Returns a `proof:CoherenceProof` asserting that a given type declaration is mutually consistent with the ring substrate at quantum level n.

Returns `200 OK` because this endpoint computes a deterministic artifact on-the-fly — same inputs always produce the same output. No resource is persisted.

get.summary: Issue a cert:InvolutionCertificate for op:neg or op:bnot
get.description: Verifies that a named operation is an involution (f(f(x)) = x for all x in R_n) and returns a `cert:InvolutionCertificate`.

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: The operation to certify. Must be an op:Involution individual.

get.summary: Compute observable metrics for a ring element
get.description: Returns RingMetric, HammingMetric, CascadeLength, CatastropheThreshold, and Commutator for a ring element.

200.description: Observable metric bundle for x in R_n

get.summary: Pairwise metric between two ring elements
get.description: Compute RingMetric, HammingMetric, or IncompatibilityMetric between two datums.

get.summary: Stratum analysis — value, delta, or trajectory
get.description: StratumValue (popcount), StratumDelta (difference), or StratumTrajectory (operation sequence).

post.summary: Path analysis — length, total variation, or winding number
post.description: Analyse a path of ring elements for PathLength, TotalVariation, or WindingNumber.

get.summary: Commutator [f,g](x) — measures non-commutativity
get.description: Compute the commutator of two operations f and g at ring element x.

post.summary: Holonomy — accumulated ring element over a closed path
post.description: Compute the accumulated transform over a closed path in the ring.

post.summary: Sliding-window stream analysis
post.description: Process a stream of ring values with sliding windows, computing stratum, hamming, and curvature metrics per window.

get.summary: List all type:PrimitiveType definitions
get.description:
Returns the catalogue of primitive type definitions: U1, U4, U8, U16, and composite type structures.

**Source:** `spec/src/namespaces/type_.rs`

200.description: Catalogue of type:PrimitiveType and composite types

get.summary: Produce a derivation:DerivationTrace — auditable step-by-step operation record
get.description:
Takes a starting value and a sequence of operations and returns a formal record of every step: input, output, formula used, and ontology reference. Also verifies the core rule holds for the original value — an independent integrity check bundled into the same response.

Use for execution audit trails. Peers can replay the trace to verify independently.

**Required parameter:** `x` — the starting value.

parameters.name: ops
parameters.description: Comma-separated operation sequence. Valid values: neg, bnot, succ, pred. Default: neg,bnot,succ.

200.description: derivation:DerivationTrace with per-step input, output, formula, and ontology reference

get.summary: Produce a trace:ExecutionTrace — exact bit state per step with Hamming drift
get.description:
Records the exact binary state of the value after each operation: decimal value, binary form, Hamming weight, bits flipped, and XOR delta from the previous step.

Non-zero `trace:totalHammingDrift` signals an anomalous operation sequence. Use alongside `/bridge/derivation` for injection detection.

**Required parameter:** `x` — the starting value.

parameters.name: ops
parameters.description: Comma-separated operation sequence. Valid values: neg, bnot, succ, pred. Default: neg,bnot.

200.description: trace:ExecutionTrace with per-frame bit state, Hamming weight, XOR delta

summary.note: zero Hamming drift — operation sequence is canonical

get.summary: Resolve any value to its canonical category — resolver:Resolution
get.description:
Sorts any value into one of four canonical categories:

- **IrreducibleSet** — odd, not a unit. Structurally unique building block.
- **ReducibleSet** — even. Factors as 2^k × odd core.
- **UnitSet** — the ring's multiplicative identity (1) or its inverse (2^n - 1).
- **ExteriorSet** — zero or the ring midpoint (2^(n-1)).

For reducible values, returns the full factor cascade. For irreducible values, confirms no further decomposition is possible.

**Required parameter:** `x` — the value to resolve.

200.description: resolver:Resolution with canonical category, factor decomposition, and partition reference

get.summary: Map a value between ring sizes — morphism:RingHomomorphism
get.description:
Maps a value from one ring size to another while preserving its structural properties.

- **Smaller target ring (projection):** strips the high bits — f(x) = x mod 2^toN.
- **Larger target ring (inclusion):** embeds the value unchanged.
- **Same size (identity):** identity map.

Returns the mapped value, which properties are preserved, injectivity and surjectivity flags, and a `morphism:CommutativityWitness` proving the map is a valid ring homomorphism.

**Required parameter:** `x` — the value to map.

parameters.name: from_n
parameters.description: Source ring size in bits. Default 8.

parameters.name: to_n
parameters.description: Target ring size in bits. Default 16 (inclusion/lossless embedding — demonstrates InclusionHomomorphism). Use to_n < from_n for projection.

200.description: morphism:RingHomomorphism with mapped value, preserved properties, and commutativity witness

get.summary: Get the formal lifecycle state for a value — state:Frame
get.description:
Returns the formal lifecycle state for an agent at value x.

- What category x belongs to and why.
- Whether x is a stable entry point (an identity or unit).
- Whether x is at a phase boundary.
- For each operation (neg, bnot, succ, pred) — where does x go, and does its category change?

Use before committing to an operation to understand the structural consequences.

**Required parameter:** `x` — the agent's current value.

200.description: state:Frame with category, entry/exit conditions, and all 4 operation transitions

get.summary: Fetch a URL and compute its UOR address
get.description: Fetches any HTTP/HTTPS resource, computes its UOR address via the Braille bijection (each byte b maps to Unicode codepoint U+2800+b), and returns a store:RetrievedObject. Does NOT write to IPFS. To persist the content, call POST /store/write.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The HTTP or HTTPS URL to fetch. Only http: and https: protocols accepted.

parameters.name: n
parameters.description: Ring size. Determines Z/(2^n)Z. Default 8 gives Z/256Z.

parameters.name: include_partition
parameters.description: If true, include partition analysis of the fetched bytes.

parameters.name: include_metrics
parameters.description: If true, include observable metrics on the first byte.

X-UOR-Address.description: First 64 chars of the computed u:Address glyph.

502.description: Remote resource unreachable or returned non-2xx.

504.description: Remote resource timed out after 15 seconds.

post.summary: Serialise a UOR object and pin it to IPFS
post.description: Takes any valid User-space or Bridge-space UOR object, wraps it in a store:StoredObject JSON-LD envelope, computes the UOR address (Braille bijection) and CIDv1 (dag-json, sha2-256), pins the envelope to IPFS, and returns the complete store:StoredObject with a store:PinRecord. Kernel-space types (u:, schema:, op:) are rejected with 422.

object.description: The UOR object to store. Must have an @type field. Must be a User-space or Bridge-space type.

pin.description: If false, compute addresses only — do not pin to IPFS (dry run).

gateway.description: Write gateway to use. 'pinata' = Pinata dedicated gateway (pinFileToIPFS, requires PINATA_JWT). 'storacha' = Storacha Network / web3.storage successor (uploadFile, requires STORACHA_KEY + STORACHA_PROOF, 5GB free tier). 'web3.storage' = legacy (degraded, use storacha instead).

label.description: Optional human-readable label. Does not affect computed addresses.

422.description: Kernel-space type rejected. Only User-space and Bridge-space types may be stored.

get.summary: Retrieve a stored UOR object from IPFS and verify integrity
get.description: Retrieves a stored UOR object by CID from an IPFS gateway, performs dual verification (CID integrity check + UOR address recomputation), and returns the full store:RetrievedObject. Set strict=true (default) to receive HTTP 409 on verification failure.

parameters.name: cid
parameters.description: A valid IPFS CID. Accepts CIDv0 (Qm...) or CIDv1 (bafy...).

schema.description: IPFS read gateway. Default is the dedicated Pinata gateway (fastest, most reliable). For Storacha-stored objects, use https://storacha.link with the directory CID and optional ?filename= parameter.

parameters.name: strict
parameters.description: If true, return 409 when store:verified is false. If false, return 200 with verified:false.

200.description: Object retrieved. Check store:verified for integrity result.

404.description: CID not found on IPFS gateway.

409.description: Retrieved but verification failed: UOR address mismatch (strict mode).

post.summary: Persist a state:Context as an IPLD DAG on IPFS
post.description: Serialises a complete UOR state:Context (a set of state:Binding objects) as a linked IPLD DAG on IPFS. Each binding becomes an individual IPFS block. The root block links to all bindings. Returns the root CID and per-binding CIDs. Designed for agent memory persistence.

address.description: Braille glyph or plain string (will be bijected).

200.description: Context persisted. Returns root CID and all binding CIDs.

400.description: Missing context, empty bindings, or invalid binding format.

get.summary: Verify a stored UOR object without returning its content
get.description: Lightweight verification endpoint. Retrieves bytes from IPFS, runs dual verification (CID integrity + UOR address recomputation), and returns only the boolean verdict and proof metadata. Content is not echoed back. Use this before processing any content received from a peer.

schema.description: IPFS read gateway. Default is the dedicated Pinata gateway. For Storacha-stored objects, use https://storacha.link with the directory CID.

parameters.name: expected_uor
parameters.description: Compare recomputed UOR address against this expected value instead of the stored one.

get.summary: List available IPFS gateways and their health status
get.description: Returns all configured IPFS gateways with their capabilities, health status (checked via the bafkqaaa identity probe per IPFS Trustless Gateway spec §7.1), and which gateway is default for read and write operations.

get.summary: SPARQL 1.1 query endpoint
get.description: SELECT, CONSTRUCT, or ASK over the UOR knowledge graph (ontology + Q0 instance graph with all 256 datums). Supports WHERE triple patterns, FILTER, LIMIT, OFFSET. Implements the uor_query tool from Section 6.4.

200.description: application/sparql-results+json — SPARQL result set with epistemic grading

post.summary: SPARQL 1.1 query (POST)
post.description: POST variant — send SPARQL query as JSON body {query: '...'} or as raw text with Content-Type: application/sparql-query.

get.summary: SHACL shapes in Turtle format
get.description: Returns the canonical SHACL shapes for UOR data validation in W3C Turtle syntax. These shapes define constraints for Datum, Derivation, Certificate, and Partition nodes.

get.summary: Q0 instance graph — all 256 datums of Z/256Z as JSON-LD
get.description: Returns the complete Q0 instance graph with 256 Datum individuals, 6 canonical derivation examples, a critical identity proof, and a partition node. Available as static file at https://uor.foundation/uor_q0.jsonld

get.summary: uor_derive — evaluate ring-arithmetic expression
get.description: Evaluate a ring-arithmetic expression with AC normalisation. Returns Grade A derivation certificate with SHA-256 derivation_id. Supports unary (neg, bnot, succ, pred), binary (xor, and, or, add, sub, mul), and literal (hex, decimal, binary) syntax.

200.description: Derivation result with derivation_id and epistemic grading

post.summary: uor_query — SPARQL query with epistemic grading
post.description: Execute a SPARQL query over the UOR knowledge graph. Each result node is annotated with derivation_id and epistemic_grade.

200.description: JSON-LD @graph array with epistemic grading per node

get.summary: uor_verify — verify a derivation_id
get.description: Verify a derivation_id against the knowledge graph. Returns verified:true (Grade A) or false (Grade D).

200.description: Verification result with cert_chain and epistemic grading

get.summary: uor_correlate — algebraic fidelity between two elements
get.description: Compute algebraic fidelity (0.0–1.0) between two ring elements using Hamming distance, XOR value, and ring distance.

200.description: Correlation result with fidelity, Hamming distance, and epistemic grading

post.summary: uor_partition — build ring partition from seed set
post.description: Build a ring partition from a seed set with closure analysis. Supports GRAPH_CLOSED (no expansion) and FIXED_POINT (expand until closed under unary ops) modes.

200.description: Partition result with cardinality, closure analysis, and epistemic grading

post.summary: Verify a ring morphism (embedding, isometry, or action)
post.description: Exhaustively verify a structure-preserving map between two quantum rings. Returns a TransformCertificate with verification result and computation trace.

200.description: Transform verification result with certificate and epistemic grading

get.summary: Verify neg or bnot as a ring isometry
get.description: Exhaustively verify that neg or bnot is an involution and metric-preserving isometry on the ring. Returns IsometryCertificate.

200.description: Isometry verification with involution check and metric preservation

get.summary: Cross-quantum value coercion (embed or project)
get.description: Coerce a value from one quantum ring to another. Embedding (lower→higher) is lossless; projection (higher→lower) may lose information.

200.description: Coerced value with target IRI and transform reference

post.summary: Issue a cert:Certificate for a derivation
post.description: Generates a verifiable cert:TransformCertificate with deterministic IRI (SHA-256 of derivation_id). Includes PROV-O provenance and epistemic grading.

get.summary: GDPR Article 20 data portability attestation
get.description: Returns a W3C Verifiable Credential attesting GDPR Article 20 data portability rights for a UOR derivation.

get.summary: SPARQL federation cardinality planning
get.description: Returns partition cardinality estimates (irreducibles, reducibles, units, exterior) for federated SPARQL query planning. Computed algebraically without network requests.

200.description: Federation plan with cardinality estimates and join strategy

post.summary: Natural-language entity resolver
post.description: Resolves natural-language entity descriptions to ring elements using constraint extraction (Hamming weight, value proximity, stratum). Returns Grade B (NL extraction is not algebraically proven).

entity.description: NL entity description e.g. 'the byte with Hamming weight 3 nearest to 42'

get.summary: Get schema.org ↔ UOR extension vocabulary
get.description: Returns the UOR extension vocabulary for schema.org: the set of additional properties (derivation_id, contentHash, attributionCertificate) that UOR adds to any schema.org object. Use this to understand the extension schema before calling POST.

200.description: Extension vocabulary with property definitions and examples

post.summary: Extend schema.org JSON-LD with UOR attribution
post.description: Takes any valid schema.org JSON-LD object and adds UOR derivation_id, contentHash, and attributionCertificate. Grade B (content hash, not algebraic proof).

get.summary: API root — redirect to /navigate
get.description: Returns the same navigation index as /navigate. Convenience alias for the root path.

get.summary: Query the oracle ledger — immutable audit log of all store operations
get.description: Returns paginated oracle ledger entries. Each entry records a store operation with derivation ID, CIDs, epistemic grade, and timestamps.

200.description: Paginated oracle ledger entries with total count

get.summary: Oracle ledger statistics — aggregate counts and metrics
get.description: Returns aggregate statistics: total operations, operations by type, objects by type, storage distribution, and time-series data.

get.summary: Algebraic correlation between two ring elements
get.description: Compute fidelity (0.0–1.0) between two ring elements using Hamming distance, XOR value, and ring distance. Equivalent to /tools/correlate.

200.description: Correlation result with fidelity, Hamming distance, XOR value, ring distance, and epistemic grading

post.summary: Derive a canonical content-addressed identity for arbitrary input
post.description: Accepts any JSON object or string, canonicalises it via URDNA2015, computes SHA-256, and returns the full UOR identity (derivation_id, CIDv1, Braille address, IPv6 ULA).

200.description: UOR identity with derivation_id, CID, Braille address, and IPv6

get.summary: Export the full UOR ontology as OWL/RDFS JSON-LD
get.description: Returns the complete UOR ontology: all classes, properties, individuals, and axioms expressed as W3C OWL 2 / RDFS. Includes all 14 namespaces.

get.summary: List all SHACL shapes for UOR ontology validation
get.description: Returns the catalogue of W3C SHACL shapes used to validate UOR data graphs. Each shape constrains a specific UOR type (schema:Datum, derivation:DerivationTrace, etc.).

get.summary: Validate a UOR object against SHACL shapes
get.description: Validates a ring element's datum representation against all applicable SHACL shapes. Returns a W3C SHACL validation report.

get.summary: Emit a UOR datum as W3C JSON-LD 1.1
get.description: Emits a fully expanded JSON-LD 1.1 document for a ring element. Includes all algebraic metadata, @context, @id, and ontology references.

get.summary: Export UOR knowledge graph in GNN-ready format
get.description: Returns the knowledge graph as a node-edge-feature structure suitable for Graph Neural Network ingestion.

200.description: GNN graph with node features, adjacency structure, and edge types

post.summary: Ground a GNN embedding back to ring elements
post.description: Maps a floating-point embedding vector to the nearest ring elements via Hamming distance minimization.

200.description: Grounded ring elements with similarity scores

post.summary: Create or retrieve a POD context for agent-scoped storage
post.description: Creates an evaluation context with a given capacity and quantum level. Returns context_id for subsequent bindings.

post.summary: Write a binding into a POD context
post.description: Stores a content-addressed binding (address → value) in the given context.

get.summary: Read a binding from a POD context
get.description: Retrieves a specific binding by context_id and address.

get.summary: List all bindings in a POD context
get.description: Returns all bindings for the given context_id with their addresses, content, and timestamps.

get.summary: Full Q0 knowledge graph as JSON-LD @graph
get.description: Returns the complete pre-computed Q0 instance graph (all 2^n datums) as a JSON-LD @graph array with full algebraic metadata per datum.

get.summary: Q0 knowledge graph statistics
get.description: Returns aggregate statistics for the Q0 instance graph: datum count, triple count, partition distribution, and namespace usage.

get.summary: Lookup a single datum in the Q0 knowledge graph
get.description: Returns the full schema:Datum for a specific value in the Q0 instance graph, including all algebraic properties and linked triples.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The datum value to look up

404.description: Datum value not found in the graph

post.summary: Register an observer agent
post.description: Registers an agent as an observer in the observer network. Assigns initial zone (COHERENCE) and records founding derivation.

get.summary: Observer network summary — zone distribution and convergence metrics
get.description: Returns aggregate network statistics: observer counts per zone, mean h-scores, convergence indicators.

post.summary: Assess an observer output for epistemic grading
post.description: Submits an output hash for epistemic assessment. Computes h-score, updates observer persistence, and may trigger zone transitions.

200.description: Assessment result with h-score and zone status

get.summary: Check network convergence status
get.description: Determines whether the observer network has reached convergence based on zone stability thresholds.

get.summary: Get observer details by agent ID
get.description: Returns full observer profile: zone, h-score mean, persistence, grade A rate, founding derivation, and field of observation.

post.summary: Register derivation attribution — provenance chain for content
post.description: Records the provenance of a derivation: who created it, when, and what it derives from. Returns an attribution certificate.

get.summary: Verify an attribution chain
get.description: Verifies the attribution chain for a derivation, checking certificate validity and provenance integrity.

get.summary: Generate royalty/revenue attribution report
get.description: Computes revenue attribution based on derivation provenance chains.

post.summary: Verify schema.org ↔ UOR coherence
post.description: Validates that a schema.org JSON-LD object's UOR attribution is consistent and the derivation chain is intact.

schema.description: Schema.org JSON-LD object with UOR attribution

post.summary: Batch pin schema.org objects to IPFS with UOR attribution
post.description: Extends and pins multiple schema.org objects in a single request.

200.description: Batch pin results with per-object CIDs and derivation IDs

get.summary: Verify a SPARQL query result against the knowledge graph
get.description: Replays a SPARQL query and verifies each result node's derivation_id. Returns epistemic grading per node.

get.summary: End-to-end conformance test
get.description: Runs the full UOR conformance test suite (test7_end_to_end.rs equivalent) against the live API and returns pass/fail per test.

200.description: Test results with pass/fail per test case

get.summary: VoID dataset descriptor
get.description: W3C VoID (Vocabulary of Interlinked Datasets) descriptor for Linked Open Data registration. Describes the Q0 knowledge graph, SPARQL endpoint, and vocabulary URIs.

200.description: VoID dataset descriptor in Turtle or JSON-LD

get.summary: Get full schema:Triad decomposition for a ring value
get.description:
Returns the triadic coordinate decomposition (datum, stratum, spectrum) for a given integer value at quantum level n. The Triad is the canonical analytical lens of the UOR framework: datum↔subject (WHAT), stratum↔predicate (HOW MUCH), spectrum↔object (WHICH bits).

**Framework mapping (schema.rs):**
- `schema:Triad` — triadic coordinate system
- `schema:datum` — big-endian byte tuple
- `schema:stratum` — popcount per byte position
- `schema:spectrum` — LSB-indexed basis elements per byte

200.description: Full schema:Triad with per-byte stratum and spectrum decomposition

get.summary: Resolve a UOR address to its stored IPFS CID
get.description: Given a UOR Braille address or derivation_id, resolves to the corresponding IPFS CID if the object has been stored. Returns the CID, gateway URL, and storage metadata.

200.description: Resolution result with CID and gateway URL

404.description: No stored object found for the given address or derivation_id

get.summary: Query the UOR knowledge graph by triple pattern
get.description: Lightweight triple-pattern query over the UOR knowledge graph. Supports subject, predicate, and object filters. Returns matching triples as a JSON-LD @graph array.

parameters.name: object
parameters.description: Filter by object IRI or literal

get.summary: Get observer zone classification
get.description: Returns the current zone (COHERENCE, DRIFT, or COLLAPSE) for a specific observer agent, along with zone transition history and h-score metrics.

get.summary: Get observer assessment history
get.description: Returns the full assessment history for an observer agent: all submitted outputs, h-scores, epistemic grades, and zone transitions over time.

200.description: Assessment history with h-scores and zone transitions

post.summary: Remediate a drifting or collapsed observer
post.description: Initiates remediation for an observer in DRIFT or COLLAPSE zone. Resets h-score accumulation and provides a remediation derivation_id for the agent to rebuild coherence.

200.description: Remediation initiated with new zone assessment

get.summary: SPARQL 1.1 query (alias for /bridge/sparql)
get.description: Top-level SPARQL endpoint — alias for /bridge/sparql. Accepts the same query parameter and returns identical SPARQL results with epistemic grading.

post.summary: SPARQL 1.1 query POST (alias for /bridge/sparql POST)
post.description: POST variant of the top-level SPARQL endpoint.

get.summary: Verify SPARQL query results (alias)
get.description: Top-level alias for SPARQL result verification with epistemic grading.

AgentKey.name: X-UOR-Agent-Key
AgentKey.description: Optional API key for elevated rate limits. Unauthenticated: 120 req/min GET, 60 req/min POST. Authenticated: 600 req/min. Obtain at https://uor.foundation.

ETag.description: Cache validation token. Deterministic: same inputs always produce the same ETag. Use with If-None-Match for conditional requests (returns 304 Not Modified on match).

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Maximum number of requests per minute for this endpoint class (120 for GET kernel, 60 for POST bridge).

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Remaining requests in the current 60-second window.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the rate limit window resets.

x.name: x
x.description: Ring element value. Must satisfy 0 ≤ x < 2^n (e.g., < 256 for n=8, < 65536 for n=16). Schema maximum is the widest valid range (n=16); actual bound enforced at runtime against `n`.

n.name: n
n.description: Ring quantum (bit-width). Selects R_n = Z/(2^n)Z. Default: 8 (R_8 = Z/256Z).

EpistemicGrading.description: Every API response includes epistemic grading fields. Grade A responses also include derivation:derivationId and derivation:resultIri.

epistemic_grade.description: UOR epistemic grade. A=Algebraically Proven (ring arithmetic + derivation ID). B=Graph-Certified (cert chain). C=Graph-Present (no cert). D=LLM-Generated (unverified).

epistemic_grade_label.description: Human-readable label for the epistemic grade.

epistemic_grade_reason.description: Explanation of why this grade was assigned and what it means for agent citation.

derivation:derivationId.description: SHA-256 content-addressed derivation identifier. Present for Grade A responses only. AC-normalised: commutative ops (xor, and, or) have arguments sorted ascending before hashing.

derivation:resultIri.description: Content-addressed IRI of the result datum. Pattern: https://uor.foundation/u/U{hex} for Q0.

Datum.description: schema:Datum — ring element with full algebraic metadata. Source: spec/src/namespaces/schema.rs. Property names use JSON-LD prefixed notation matching the @context in the response envelope — use string-key access in typed languages (e.g., obj["schema:value"]).

schema:value.description: Integer value in [0, 2^n). JSON-LD wire key: 'schema:value'.

schema:quantum.description: Ring bit-width n. JSON-LD wire key: 'schema:quantum'.

schema:stratum.description: Number of set bits (popcount / Hamming weight). JSON-LD wire key: 'schema:stratum'.

schema:spectrum.description: Binary representation string, zero-padded to n digits. JSON-LD wire key: 'schema:spectrum'.

schema:glyph.description: u:Address — content-addressable Braille identifier. JSON-LD wire key: 'schema:glyph'.

u:glyph.description: Braille character(s) from Unicode Braille block (U+2800–U+28FF). Wire key: 'u:glyph'.

WitnessData.description: proof:WitnessData — algebraic witness for critical identity verification. Wire keys use JSON-LD prefixed notation.

CriticalIdentityProofResponse.description: proof:CriticalIdentityProof — full algebraic verification with derivation steps

@context.description: JSON-LD context URL. Resolves to https://uor.foundation/contexts/uor-v1.jsonld — fetch once, cache permanently.

CoherenceProofResponse.description: proof:CoherenceProof — universal verification across all 2^n ring elements

type_definition.description: type:TypeDefinition to verify coherence for. Minimum: {"@type": "type:PrimitiveType", "type:bitWidth": 8}

type:bitWidth.description: Required for type:PrimitiveType. Specifies the ring quantum n.

n.description: Ring quantum (bit-width). If omitted, taken from type_definition.type:bitWidth, then defaults to 8.

UnaryOpResult.description: Result of a named op:UnaryOp individual applied to x

BinaryOpResult.description: Result of a named op:BinaryOp individual applied to (x, y)

OpComputeResponse.description: Results of all ring operations applied to x (and y for binary ops)

OpListResponse.description: Complete catalogue of all 12 named op/ individuals

input.description: UTF-8 string to encode as a u:Address

PartitionRequest.description: Exactly one of `type_definition` or `input` must be provided.

oneOf.title: TypeDefinitionPartition
oneOf.description: Full ring partition for a type:TypeDefinition

oneOf.title: InputStringPartition
oneOf.description: Per-byte partition analysis of a UTF-8 string

input.description: UTF-8 string for per-byte partition analysis

PartitionResponse.description: partition:Partition with four disjoint, exhaustive components summing to 2^n

InvolutionCertificateResponse.description: cert:InvolutionCertificate — proof that f(f(x)) = x for all x in R_n

ObservableMetricsResponse.description: observable:MetricBundle — RingMetric, HammingMetric, CascadeLength, CatastropheThreshold, Commutator for x in R_n

observable:cascadeLength.description: trailing zero count — depth of 2-adic factorization

observable:commutator.description: Commutator measurement — 0 for commutative ring operations

TypeListResponse.description: Catalogue of type:PrimitiveType and composite type definitions

NavigationIndex.description: Complete navigation index — all endpoints, namespace map, reading order

DerivationTraceResponse.description: derivation:DerivationTrace — auditable, step-by-step operation record. Source: spec/src/namespaces/derivation.rs

ExecutionTraceResponse.description: trace:ExecutionTrace — bit-level execution trace per operation step. Non-zero totalHammingDrift signals injection. Source: spec/src/namespaces/trace.rs

injection_example.description: Worked example showing canonical (drift=0) vs anomalous (drift≠0) sequences

trace:totalHammingDrift.description: Net change in Hamming weight from start to end state. 0 = canonical, non-zero = anomalous / injection signal.

ResolutionResponse.description: resolver:Resolution — canonical category classification with factor decomposition. Source: spec/src/namespaces/resolver.rs

RingHomomorphismResponse.description: morphism:RingHomomorphism — structure-preserving map between ring sizes. Source: spec/src/namespaces/morphism.rs

StateFrameResponse.description: state:Frame — formal agent lifecycle state with category, entry/exit conditions, and all operation transitions. Source: spec/src/namespaces/state.rs

param.description: The parameter that caused the error (for 400 responses)

StoreStoredObject.description: A UOR object that has been serialised to JSON-LD and stored on IPFS. Carries both a u:Address (semantic identity) and a store:Cid (storage identity).

payload.description: The original UOR object as stored.

StoreRetrievedObject.description: The result of retrieving a stored UOR object from IPFS, with dual verification results.

store:verified.description: true = verified, false = integrity failure, null = indeterminate (no reference address).

content.description: The retrieved content. Present on /store/read, absent on /store/verify.

StorePinRecord.description: Gateway-specific pin record. For Storacha: includes directoryCid, gatewayUrl, filename. For Pinata: includes IpfsHash, PinSize, Timestamp.

store:gatewayUrl.description: Direct HTTPS URL to retrieve the stored file.

store:directoryCid.description: Storacha only. UnixFS directory CID (bafy...). Append filename to gateway URL for retrieval.

store:filename.description: Filename used when uploading. Required for Storacha subdomain gateway reads.

u:glyph.description: Braille string. Each character encodes one byte via U+2800+byte.

u:length.description: Number of bytes (= number of Braille glyphs).

example.error: Parameter 'x' must be an integer in [0, 65535]

MethodNotAllowed.description: HTTP method not supported for this endpoint. Check the Allow response header for permitted methods.

Allow.description: Comma-separated list of allowed HTTP methods for this path (e.g., GET, OPTIONS)

example.error: Method DELETE not allowed. Allowed: GET, OPTIONS

UnsupportedMediaType.description: Request body must have Content-Type: application/json.

RateLimited.description: Rate limit exceeded. Retry after 60 seconds.

NotImplemented.description: This endpoint requires the Rust conformance suite (full dihedral factorization) and is not yet available in the cloud API. See https://github.com/UOR-Foundation/UOR-Framework for the local implementation.

InternalServerError.description: Unexpected server-side error. The UOR kernel computation itself is deterministic; this indicates an infrastructure failure. Retry with exponential backoff.
